mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the prior month signals easier discounting and lower recession risk, which lifts equity valuations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not higher than baseline when FEDFUNDS trends down, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above 9.0% with further monthly deterioration identifies labor-market stress that raises default and earnings-revision risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20d returns more than 60% of the time, or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 0.5 standard deviations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates stronger payroll income and retail demand, which supports revenue growth expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS-RSAFS improvement does not outperform either series alone in walk-forward tests, or if 60d returns after joint strength are not positive in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO reflects improving industrial output and capacity utilization, which signals cyclical earnings momentum and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 3 consecutive monthly prints
failure_condition: Reject if three-month INDPRO uptrends are followed by negative or flat 20d SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the signal's IC is not statistically distinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A jump in HOUST above its recent trend indicates housing demand and credit-sensitive activity are recovering, which precedes positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST exceeds its 6-month moving average by 5%
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakouts do not lead to higher 60d SPX returns than the unconditional sample, or if the conditional median return is non-positive.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation stays elevated while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, real rates become more negative and policy stays accommodative, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation is above its 12-month average while FEDFUNDS < 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction is not stronger than either proxy alone, or if triggered 5d returns are not positive relative to baseline in at least 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A continued decline in PAYEMS means payroll creation is weakening, so earnings expectations and cyclical risk appetite fall, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 20d SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the signal loses significance in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession rebound, rising GDPC1 combined with stronger RSAFS indicates broadening final demand, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if GDP and sales strength do not jointly improve 60d forward returns versus either series alone, or if the joint signal turns negative in more than one rolling 2-year period.
